text: When you write code, you will often face bugs that prevent your program from working correctly. The first step is identifying bugs by noticing unexpected behavior or error messages. A systematic approach is essential: instead of guessing, break the problem into small parts and test each one. Use debugging tools like breakpoints to pause your code and inspect variables. Another useful strategy is logging—adding print statements to track what your program does step by step. By writing messages to the console, you can see where values go wrong. With patience and a clear method, you can solve most errors efficiently.
